projects.title: Merchant Risk Signal Dashboard
addresses_gaps: SQL skills claimed but not demonstrated in a bullet (low confidence)
Amplitude/analytics tools claimed but not demonstrated (low confidence)
projects.business_problem: Fraud/risk PMs need to self-serve merchant risk signals (chargeback trends, onboarding drop-off, fraud-score distribution) without waiting on data science for every ad hoc question — mirrors the JD's own call-out that this role 'pulls your own data for prioritization decisions.'
features: SQL-backed queries surfacing merchant risk cohorts by fraud-score band
A dashboard showing chargeback rate trend, onboarding funnel drop-off, and false-positive rate over time
Threshold-based alerting when a risk metric crosses a defined bound
A written case study documenting 2-3 specific SQL queries and the prioritization decisions they informed
ai_opportunities: LLM-generated plain-English weekly risk summary from the underlying SQL results
Anomaly detection flagging unusual spikes in chargeback or false-positive rate for follow-up
apis: A public/synthetic transaction dataset (e.g. IEEE-CIS Fraud Detection on Kaggle)
Amplitude free tier for event-level tracking
Optional LLM API for the summary feature
tech_stack: Streamlit or a lightweight BI layer for the dashboard
kpis: Number of distinct self-serve questions the dashboard answers without an analyst
Time-to-insight for a sample question (e.g. 'which merchant cohort had the biggest fraud spike this month')
Whether the written case study clearly traces a decision back to a specific query
prd_outline: Problem Statement — risk PMs lack self-serve visibility into fraud/chargeback trends
Goals — enable N self-serve queries; cut time-to-insight from days to minutes
Non-goals — not a replacement for the full data-science fraud model
Target Users — risk/fraud PM, trust & safety analyst
Requirements — SQL-backed cohort queries, trend dashboard, threshold alerting
Success Metrics — time-to-insight, decisions traced to a specific query
Risks — synthetic data may not reflect real-world fraud patterns
projects.mvp: A single dashboard view answering one concrete question end-to-end (e.g. 'which merchant onboarding cohort has the highest 30-day chargeback rate') built on a synthetic transaction dataset, with the underlying SQL query and resulting decision documented in a short case-study writeup.
future_enhancements: Correlate risk signals with a second data source (e.g. support ticket volume)
Self-serve query builder UI for non-SQL stakeholders
projects.estimated_build_time: 1-2 weekends for the MVP dashboard plus case study
projects.priority_reasoning: Closes two LOW-confidence gaps at once using the exact domain (fraud/risk) and exact JD language ('pull your own data for prioritization decisions') — highest leverage per unit of build effort.

projects.title: RBI/PCI-DSS Compliance-to-PRD Translator
addresses_gaps: Regulatory translation (RBI/PCI-DSS) evidenced but under-specified, no concrete example (medium confidence)
projects.business_problem: Product teams in regulated fintech repeatedly need to turn dense regulatory text (RBI circulars, PCI-DSS requirements) into concrete, engineering-ready product requirements — a slow, manual process today.
target_users: Compliance leads partnering with product teams
features: Paste a regulatory excerpt; the tool extracts discrete obligations
Maps each obligation to a suggested product requirement (e.g. a retention obligation becomes a data-retention policy field)
Outputs a mini one-page PRD draft from the mapped obligations
A small library of 3-4 worked examples (one RBI circular, one PCI-DSS requirement) as the case-study centerpiece
ai_opportunities: LLM-powered extraction of discrete obligations from unstructured regulatory text
LLM-drafted first-pass PRD requirement language from each obligation, clearly labeled as a draft for human review
apis: An LLM API (Anthropic/OpenAI) for extraction and drafting
Publicly available RBI circulars and the public PCI-DSS standard as source text
tech_stack: A simple web UI (Streamlit or a small Next.js app)
No database required for the MVP
kpis: Extraction accuracy (precision/recall) on the 3-4 worked examples vs. manual identification
Time to go from raw regulatory text to a draft PRD requirements section
prd_outline: Problem Statement — regulatory-to-product translation is slow and manual today
Goals — cut first-draft PRD time for a regulatory requirement from hours to minutes
Non-goals — this tool provides drafting assistance only, never legal/compliance sign-off
Target Users — fintech PMs, compliance leads
Requirements — obligation extraction, requirement drafting, worked-example library
Success Metrics — extraction accuracy on worked examples, time saved
Risks — regulatory misinterpretation if used without human review; must be clearly labeled as a drafting aid
projects.mvp: A single working example end-to-end: paste one real RBI KYC circular excerpt, get back 3-5 extracted obligations and a draft PRD requirements section, with a clear disclaimer that this is a drafting aid, not compliance guidance.
future_enhancements: Expand the worked-example library to PCI-DSS and GDPR
Add a review/approval workflow for compliance sign-off
projects.estimated_build_time: 1 weekend for the MVP (single worked example end-to-end)
projects.priority_reasoning: Targets the MEDIUM-confidence regulatory-translation gap with a project that IS the skill being practiced — the resulting artifact becomes interview-ready evidence, but it addresses a less severe gap than Project 1.

projects.title: Public PRD Case Study: KYC Onboarding Redesign
addresses_gaps: Written communication (PRDs, leadership presentations) evidenced but under-specified (medium confidence)
projects.business_problem: The resume shows PRD-writing responsibility but no artifact demonstrates communication quality or leadership-level framing — hiring teams for senior/leadership-track roles often want to see actual writing, not just a bullet claiming it.
target_users: Hiring managers and interview panels evaluating communication skill directly
features: A polished, publicly shareable one-page PRD reusing the candidate's real, existing KYC-flow-redesign achievement (already on the resume — authentic, not speculative)
Reframed as a leadership-style document: problem framing, options considered, recommendation, success metrics
A one-paragraph 'how I'd present this to leadership' summary
tech_stack: A published doc (Notion page, PDF, or personal site) — no engineering build needed
kpis: Qualitative only: reviewer feedback on clarity and leadership-level framing
prd_outline: Success Metrics (the real 3-day→18-hour and 22%/31% outcomes already on the resume)
Leadership Summary — a one-paragraph executive framing
projects.mvp: A single one-page PRD case study for the KYC redesign, published somewhere linkable (Notion or a personal site) and referenced from the resume/LinkedIn.
future_enhancements: A second case study for the fraud scoring model achievement
A short video walkthrough reusable for interview prep
projects.estimated_build_time: 1 evening — a writing exercise, not a build
projects.priority_reasoning: Lowest build cost and reuses already-verified achievements rather than requiring new work, but ranked last because it targets a MEDIUM-confidence gap that was already partially evidenced, versus the other two projects' LOWER-confidence, higher-severity gaps.

gaps_not_addressed: Experience managing or mentoring other PMs — a people-management/mentoring gap is an interpersonal experience gap a solo portfolio project cannot credibly simulate. Closing it requires an actual mentoring relationship (e.g. volunteering to mentor a junior/associate PM) or adding real informal mentoring to the resume if it already exists — this is Career Strategy / Learning & Certification Agent territory, not a portfolio project.

PM certification — a credential isn't something a portfolio project substitutes for. If pursued, this is a direct enrollment decision (Learning & Certification Agent territory), and the JD itself marks this low-priority for this specific application.
human_readable_summary:
## Portfolio Recommendations: Senior PM — Risk & Fraud @ Razorpay

**3 projects proposed, all in the fraud/risk/payments domain** to stay directly relevant to the target role rather than generic portfolio filler.

1. **Merchant Risk Signal Dashboard** (highest priority) — closes both the SQL and Amplitude evidence gaps at once, using the JD's own language about self-serve data pulls.
2. **RBI/PCI-DSS Compliance-to-PRD Translator** — turns the regulatory-translation gap into a project that IS the skill being demonstrated.
3. **Public PRD Case Study** — lowest effort (one evening), reuses a real, already-verified achievement rather than inventing new work.

**2 gaps deliberately not portfolio'd:** PM mentoring/management experience and the PM certification. Neither can be credibly simulated or substituted by a solo project — see `gaps_not_addressed` for why, and what to do instead.
limitations: Build-time estimates are approximate; actual effort depends on the candidate's familiarity with the specific tools chosen.
The compliance-translator project produces drafting aids only — its own description must clearly label output as non-authoritative, not real compliance guidance.
Portfolio projects can only address demonstrable-skill gaps; experience-based gaps (mentoring) and credentials (certification) are explicitly out of scope — see gaps_not_addressed.
